page: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a substantial 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated mathematical and matrix operations.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong performance in memory bandwidth, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 210.27 GB/sec and write speeds at 92.5 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates high efficiency in data compression tasks, achieving 31.42 GB/sec in single-threaded gzip compression and 196.62 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. While it excels in static web serving throughput at 53.75 GB/sec and small-model LLM text generation, performance drops significantly for larger models, with text generation for a 70B parameter model falling to a weak 2.71 tokens/sec. Additionally, the instance lacks local storage, requiring remote premium storage attachments.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D32ns_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require high network throughput and steady CPU performance without the premium cost of dedicated GPU hardware. The inclusion of AMX instructions provides a cost-effective alternative for lightweight CPU-based machine learning inference. This server is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, network-optimized application servers, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized, general-purpose virtual machine utilizing an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instructions. The instance delivers strong performance in memory bandwidth, multi-threaded decompression, and static web serving, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. However, it lacks local storage and exhibits weak performance on large-scale 70B LLM inference. Qualitatively, the server provides balanced resource density and high network throughput, making it a cost-effective choice for enterprise application hosting, web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and AMX support, delivering strong memory bandwidth and web serving performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ns_v6 is a network-optimized VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, built for web serving and balanced workloads.
tagline: A network-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory density for enterprise applications and web serving.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix math.
Strong memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression capabilities.
Optimized for high-throughput static web serving and network-heavy workloads.
Lacks local storage, relying entirely on remote premium storage options.
